title: Quoting Identifier Options for SQL Code Generation
story: As a data migration specialist, I want to choose a quoting identifier style for my SQL output, so that the generated code uses the correct syntax for my target database platform.
description:
Add a dropdown to select the quoting identifier used in generated SQL code. Different databases require different quoting conventions for identifiers:

- **Double Quotes**: `"SimpleLead"."Last Name" AS "LastName"` (Snowflake, PostgreSQL)
- **Brackets**: `[SimpleLead].[Last Name] AS [LastName]` (SQL Server)
- **Backticks**: `` `SimpleLead`.`Last Name` AS `LastName` `` (MySQL)
- **None**: No quoting applied

The selected quoting style applies to all object and field references in the generated SQL output.
acceptance_criteria: Dropdown presents options: Double Quotes, Brackets, Backticks, None
Selected quoting style is applied to all object and field identifiers in SQL output
Double quotes used for Snowflake/PostgreSQL style
Square brackets used for SQL Server style
None option generates identifiers without any quoting
ux_changes: New quoting identifier dropdown added to the SQL code generation modal with options for Double Quotes, Brackets, Backticks, and None.
middleware_changes: SQL generation logic updated to apply the selected quoting style to all identifier references in the output.
implementation_notes: Initially defaulted to double quotes for all output. The dropdown was added as a UI control that dynamically re-renders the SQL with the selected quoting style.
comment_summary: Developer initially hardcoded double quotes for all SQL and asked if a selection dropdown was needed. Confirmed that the dropdown would be valuable. Developer implemented and deployed to staging for review.
